title: Hosackia yollabolliensis

The genus Hosackia is a group of flowering plants that are typically found in the Western United States. The specific epithet yollabolliensis indicates its native range in Yolla Bolly-Middle Eel Wilderness in California. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Hosackia yollabolliensis has characteristics typical of other legumes, including the ability to fix nitrogen from the air.
